Short_Description: The Alamo Canyon to Valle Seco Lasso is a 13.5-mile bike route through the western portion of Valles Caldera National Preserve. This ride features 1,800 feet of elevation gain and a speedy final descent through the park's densest concentration of geothermal features.

Title: Bike the Redondo Foothills Loop
Short_Description: The Redondo Foothills Loop is a moderately strenuous, 9.5-mile bike route beginning in the Cabin District. This ride features flowy descents, 771 feet of climbing, and great views of Valle Grande.

Title: Fish in Valle San Antonio
Short_Description: San Antonio Creek is famous throughout New Mexico for wild brown trout and beautiful scenery. Reserve your backcountry vehicle pass to fish this stream seasonally from May 15 - November 15.

Title: Fish San Antonio Creek
Short_Description: San Antonio Creek is famous throughout New Mexico for wild brown trout and beautiful scenery. Reserve your backcountry vehicle pass to fish this stream seasonally from May 15 - November 15.

Title: Fish the Headwaters of San Antonio Creek
Short_Description: San Antonio Creek is famous throughout New Mexico for wild brown trout and beautiful scenery. Reserve your backcountry vehicle pass and drive to Valle Toledo to fish this stream's headwaters!

Title: Hike the Garita Trail
Short_Description: The Garita Trail is a 7.6-mile round-trip hike to the northern rim of Valles Caldera. It takes 3-4 hours to complete. Pets are not permitted.

Title: Hike the Cerro Abrigo Loop
Short_Description: This 7.2-mile (11.6 km) loop gently ascends a lava dome and then contours around it, offering great views of the eastern half of the caldera. This hike takes 3-4 hours to complete. Pets are not permitted.

Title: Hike the Sulphur & Alamo Canyon Loop
Short_Description: The Sulphur & Alamo Canyon Loop is a strenuous, 12.2-mile trail through Valles Caldera's geothermal areas. It takes approximately 6 hours to complete. Pets are not permitted.

Title: Hike the Cerro Seco Loop
Short_Description: The Cerro Seco Loop is a strenuous, 11.2-mile round-trip hike that circles a lava dome with fantastic views of the caldera's north rim. It takes 5-6 hours to complete. Pets are not permitted.

Title: Hike the Northwest Rim Trail
Short_Description: At 18.6 miles round-trip, the Northwest Rim Trail is ideal for trail runners. At an average hiking pace, it takes 9-10 hours to complete. Pets are not permitted.

Title: Hike the Indios Creek Trail
Short_Description: The Indios Creek Trail is a 7.8-mile round-trip hike that follows a creek to the northern boundary of the park. It takes about 4 hours to complete. Pets are not permitted.

Title: Hike to Sulphur Springs
Short_Description: Sulphur Springs—a site bubbling with geothermal activity—is accessed via a 1-mile (1.6-km) round-trip hike on the Sulphur Creek Trail. This hike takes approximately 30-60 minutes. Pets are not permitted.

Title: Fish the East Fork Headwaters
Short_Description: Hike or bike the Rincon Trail to the East Fork headwaters, where wild rainbow trout and browns predominate.

Title: Fish the East Fork Jemez River
Short_Description: A picturesque and meandering stream, wild rainbow trout and browns can be found in the headwaters of the East Fork of the Jemez River. Rio Grande suckers and Rio Grande chubs predominate elsewhere.

Title: Hike Jordan Pond Path
Short_Description: The trail follows the shore of Jordan Pond, providing iconic views of steep, glacially sculpted mountains and the glacially carved valley that is now Jordan Pond.

Title: Plaza Access in the Fort
Short_Description: The public is invited inside the reconstruction of Bent’s Old Fort, entering through the zaguan gate and into the plaza. Park Rangers will be present to explain the features of the fort and its 20th century reconstruction. Entry into the reconstructed fort is limited to the central plaza, except on guided tours. For your safety, respect all closures and restrictions. Guided tours provide access into additional areas.

Title: Fort McHenry Movie
Short_Description: Fort McHenry's introductory film is one of the most popular activities at the park.

Title: Visit the Meriam House
Short_Description: The Meriam House is closed for the winter. Join us again in 2025 when we invite you to come explore the house and learn about the fateful events of April 19, 1775, and the generations of 3 families that once lived here.

Title: Tour the Thomas Stone House
Short_Description: Join a ranger for a 30 minute guided tour of the Thomas Stone House to explore Thomas Stone's life as a planter, a lawyer, a politician, and a signer of the Declaration of Independence.

Title: Western National Parks Store at Bent's Old Fort
Short_Description: The park store is operated by Western National Parks, an official non-profit partner of the National Park Service dedicated to supporting the educational mission of Bent's Old Fort National Historic Site. The park store has a wide range of books, maps, travel guides, media, educational games, posters, and other items available for retail sale.

Title: Take a Boat Ride on the Canal
Short_Description: Today, visitors can enjoy life on the canal during a free replica canal launch boat program at Williamsport. During the late 19th and early 20th century, 30-foot battery and steam powered launch boats traveled the canal for leisure and business.

Title: Wildlife Watching - Riverbend Park
Short_Description: Step onto the Potomac Heritage Trail at Riverbend Park and look out for bald eagles, osprey, belted kingfishers, mergansers, and more!

Title: War on the Chesapeake
Short_Description: This free 45-minute ranger program focuses on the Chesapeake Campaign of the War of 1812. Follow a dramatic story of devastating British raids and the American response, culminating in Baltimore at North Point, Hampstead Hill and Fort McHenry. Discover how Chesapeake victories ignited a surge of national pride, making Fort McHenry an enduring symbol of American resilience.

Title: Horseback Riding at Saratoga
Short_Description: Ride on the historic grounds of the Saratoga Battlefield where in 1777, a large British invasion force met an equally large American army here. Visitors may bring their own horses to the battlefield unit and make use of our 7+ mile (round trip) Horse Trail.

Title: Walk the Stout Memorial Grove Trail
Short_Description: A majestic grove of redwoods next to the Smith River in the northern part of the park. This trail requires some planning about how you will access it. Hours can be spent on a summer's day relaxing on the banks of the Smith River.

Title: Fife and Drum Music Demonstration
Short_Description: Music played an important role in the daily lives of soldiers garrisoned at Fort McHenry. A soldier's entire day would revolve around the sounds of fifes and drums. Join living historians in the famous "Fort McHenry Guard Fife and Drum Corps" as they play period music and give a history of music's role at Fort McHenry.

Title: Walk the Fern Canyon Loop Trail
Short_Description: A unique, beautiful, and popular location in the park. Getting here does require some planning, reservations are required May 15th through September 15th (available online), there are two (2) stream crossings that you have to drive through, and your feet will get wet. Definitely an adventure!

Title: Battle of Baltimore
Short_Description: This 45–60-minute, ranger-led walk guides visitors from the Chesapeake Map to the Historic Star Fort. Along the way, visitors will hear about how the fort withstood a 25-hour storm of rockets and exploding shells, blocking the British fleet from entering Baltimore Harbor. This defiant stand, witnessed by lawyer Francis Scott Key, inspired the verses that became “The Star-Spangled Banner".

Title: Stroll the Simpson-Reed Trail
Short_Description: An easy walk through beautiful old-growth redwood forests in the northern part of the parks. Limited parking, RV's and trailers not permitted.

Title: Hike the Boy Scout Tree Trail
Short_Description: A moderate hike with some steep grades and switchbacks.

Title: Paddle the Baltimore Blueway
Short_Description: Choose from 30 miles of trails in the Inner Harbor, Outer Harbor, Middle Branch, and Patapsco River. Imagine yourself gliding alongside historic ships, seeing the skyline from the water, and paddling around Fort McHenry. Take a selfie with Mr. Trash Wheel or immerse yourself in nature surrounded by wetlands and wildlife. Nowhere else in the Chesapeake Bay can you find such a diverse range of awe-inspiring paddling experiences within a 3-mile radius.

Title: Go Tide Pooling at Enderts Beach
Short_Description: Tidepooling at Enderts Beach will bring you experience some incredible coastlines and give you a glimpse into life at the edge of the ocean. For some, its like seeing science fiction come to life. Check the tides, and take a few hours to make the hike to the tide pools and back.

Title: Ten Thousand Islands Boat Tour
Short_Description: Experience the natural beauty of Everglades National Park's Ten Thousand Islands with a boat tour on Chokoloskee Bay.

Title: Wildlife Watching - Government Island
Short_Description: Located in Stafford, VA, this section of trail offers a boardwalk through a freshwater wetland and marsh habitat and a loop around a forested island. There are abundant opportunities for bird watching and wildlife viewing all times of year.

Title: Try en plein air painting at Mount Rainier National Park
Short_Description: With just a little planning you can create art anywhere. Take time to really experience your favorite viewpoint by painting a picture in the park.

Title: Full Moon Hikes at Mount Rainier
Short_Description: Trail options and safety tips when hiking under a full moon at Longmire, Paradise, and Box Canyon at Mount Rainier National Park.

Title: Ride a Bike in Yosemite Valley
Short_Description: Cycling is a great way to experience the park and allows you to cover more ground during your time here. You can rent a bike, bring your own, or borrow one through the bike share program (2-hour limit). Hand-crank and tandem bicycles are also available for rent. Over 12 miles of paved bike paths are available in Yosemite Valley (speed limit is 15 mph). In addition, bicyclists can ride on regular roads (if they obey traffic laws).

Title: Wildlife Watching - Oldtown C&O Canal
Short_Description: Head down the C&O Canal NHP towpath in Oldtown, Maryland into a rare ecosystem mirroring that of the West. Spot a variety of wildlife and dragonfly species in this secluded hotspot.

Title: Wildlife Watching - Point Lookout State Park
Short_Description: At the terminus of the PHT lays Point Lookout, a Maryland State Park known for its history, but also its birding. Birding enthusiasts can catch sight of rare waterfowl during the winter months and in spring and summer view the migration of a variety of shorebirds.

Title: Hiking in Yosemite
Short_Description: From a short walk to the base of a waterfall to a multi-day hike deep into the Yosemite Wilderness, taking a hike is one of the best ways to experience Yosemite's spectacular scenery. Yosemite has an extensive network of over 800 miles of hiking trails.

Title: Scenic Driving in Yosemite
Short_Description: All of the roads in Yosemite National Park are scenic, but the most famous scenic drive is along the Tioga Road, a 46-mile (62 km) drive from Crane Flat to Tioga Pass. The road is typically open from late May or early June through sometime in November.

Title: Watch the "Spirit of Yosemite" Film
Short_Description: The park film, The Spirit of Yosemite, is shown daily on the hour and the half hour in the theater behind the Yosemite Exploration Center. The first showing is at 9:30am, and the last showing is at 4pm.

Title: Learn Art in the Park
Short_Description: Yosemite has a long history of inspiring visitors to create art. From world-renowned artists like Ansel Adams and Chiura Obata to everyday people who love art for fun, pausing to create something beautiful is a special way to experience Yosemite's towering cliffs, dramatic waterfalls, enormous trees, and granite high country.

Title: Wildlife Safety in Yosemite
Short_Description: Yosemite National Park supports more than 400 species of vertebrates including fish, amphibians, reptiles, birds, and mammals. The high diversity of species is the result of diverse habitats in Yosemite that are largely intact. The park’s rich habitats range from thick foothill chaparral to conifer forests to expanses of alpine rock. Animals feel at home in each location.

Title: Become a Yosemite Junior Ranger
Short_Description: Kids can become a Yosemite Junior Ranger by taking a self-guided handbook with them as they travel around the park and complete the activities that are the best fit for them. The Yosemite Junior Ranger handbook is available for free at visitor centers. When you are done, share your booklet with a park ranger at any Yosemite visitor center during operating hours to receive your badge.

Title: Visit the Yosemite History Center
Short_Description: Go back to a time of horse-drawn wagons, a covered bridge, and rustic cabins. The Yosemite History Center is a collection of historic buildings where outdoor interpretive signs tell the stories of people who moved here from around the world and shaped the park’s development. During the summer, visit exhibits inside the Chinese Laundry and the Acting Superintendent's Office, take a ride on our horse-drawn wagon, or watch blacksmiths forge iron tools on a coal forge!

Title: Visit the Yosemite Exploration Center
Short_Description: The Yosemite Exploration Center features interactive displays and exhibits highlighting Yosemite's many stories and resources. Bring the family and learn more about Yosemite's history, the people who originally called this place home, as well as more about meadows, animals, and sequoias!

Title: Go Mountain Biking in Cuyahoga Valley
Short_Description: The East Rim Trail system has stunning views, varied terrain, exciting obstacles, and an element of adventure for intermediate mountain bikers.

Title: Hike Village Green to Sieur de Monts with Island Explorer Bus
Short_Description: Beginning at the Village Green in Bar Harbor and ending at the Sieur de Mont Area this hike includes the Great Meadow Loop, the Hemlock Road Trail, and the Jesup Path. With virtually no elevation gain it traverses a Bar Harbor neighborhood, crosses the Great Meadow, and finishes with a boardwalk at the Nature Center.

Title: Hike Pemetic Summit with Island Explorer Bus
Short_Description: The hike up and over Pemetic takes hikers to the shores of both Bubble and Jordan Pond and summits Pemetic Mountain. Nearly 360-degree views look out over Cadillac, Sargent and Penobscot peaks, the Atlantic Ocean and the Cranberry Isles. Hiking this peak with use of the Island Explorer Bus offers hikers a bus-stop-only starting point for a strenuous hike with minimal mileage.

Title: Hike Double Bubble Nubble Loop with Island Explorer
Short_Description: The trek to summit both Bubbles and Conners Nubble summits three peaks and accesses iconic park locations. Hikers will be treated to views of lakes and mountains while exploring woodlands. By hiking with the use of the Island Explorer Bus, these trails are accessible in a unique way and helps hikers overcome the crowds while exploring popular viewpoints.

Title: Hike Gorge/Cadillac Mountain North Ridge Loop with Island Explorer Bus
Short_Description: This is a great option for hiking to the summit of Cadillac Mountain. It follows Kebo Brook which cuts deeply through the granite bedrock with views of steep canyons walls from the gorge below. The final portion of the path ascends a steep rock face and gives hikers vistas of Dorr Mountain, Frenchman Bay, the Atlantic Ocean, as well the deeply cut gorge itself. The summit of Cadillac Mountain provides a 360-degree view out over Mount Desert Island.

Title: Burma Trail
Short_Description: Burma Trail is a seven-mile-long footpath that spans the Otis Pike Fire Island High Dune Wilderness Area from Watch Hill to Smith Point and allows access to Fire Island's Wilderness for hikers, photographers, birders, campers, anglers, hunters, and others.

Title: Hike the Acadia Mountain Loop with Island Explorer Bus
Short_Description: Acadia Mountain Trail follows up the east and west side of the mountain with views of the surrounding mountains and bodies of water. With the Island Explorer bus, visitors won't need to park along the road and can ride to the trailhead stress-free.

Title: Tour the 1797 Manor House
Short_Description: Belle Grove offers guests tours of the region's premier authentic antebellum plantation. Tours last 45 minutes to one hour. Tickets for tours do not require advanced reservations.

Title: Hike Sieur de Monts to Jesup Path with Island Explorer Bus
Short_Description: The trails from Sieur de Monts to Jesup Path are located adjacent to the Nature Center and the Wild Gardens of Acadia in a wooded area.

Title: Hike Sieur de Monts to Sand Beach Trek with Island Explorer
Short_Description: The trek from Sieur de Monts to Sand Beach connects four trails and summits Champlain Mountain. Hikers will be treated to views of lakes, mountains and ocean while exploring woodlands, ponds and bare summits. By hiking with the use of the Island Explorer Bus, these trails are transformed from out-and-back hikes to a scenic trek across Acadia National Park.

Title: Hike Beech Cliff Trail with Island Explorer Bus
Short_Description: The Beech Cliff Trail climbs a steep rock face from the southern end of Echo Lake to the top of the cliffs. Continue north along the short and fun Beech Cliff Loop to be treated to even more views from the top of the cliffs. The descent is more gradual and goes down a forested area via the Canada Cliffs Trail back to the beginning of the hike.

Title: Watch the Park Orientation Film
Short_Description: The 19-minute park orientation film Determined to Persevere: The Valley Forge Encampment is shown daily on the hour and the half-hour in the Meeting Room. The first screening is at 9:30 AM and the final screening is at 4:30 PM.

Title: A Forgotten Civil War: The Revolution in the Mohawk Valley
Short_Description: Explore this exhibit and meet the people of the Mohawk Valley. Discover how the American Revolutionary War affected their lives. Who really won or lost? What did "victory" cost them? Was it worth it? You be the judge.

Title: Exhibits, Artifacts, & Park Films
Short_Description: There are several videos available at the park visitor center that detail the history of the American Revolution in the Mohawk Valley.

Title: Watch the Short Films
Short_Description: Four short films play regularly in the museum exhibit located in the Visitor Center at Valley Forge.

Title: Listen to the Audio Tour
Short_Description: The Audio Tour is available for purchase as an audio CD, a downloadable MP3 file, or for streaming using a smartphone or tablet. You can purchase and listen to the Audio Tour by visiting The Encampment Store website.

Title: Be a Valley Forge Junior Ranger
Short_Description: Complete in the in-park Junior Ranger program by picking up a copy of the Junior Ranger Activity Booklet from a ranger at the visitor center.

Title: Explore the Valley Forge Encampment Museum Exhibit
Short_Description: Explore the sights, sounds, and stories of the Valley Forge encampment in the brand-new museum exhibit located at the Visitor Center at Valley Forge!

Title: Be a Valley Forge B.A.R.K. Ranger
Short_Description: Learn the four B.A.R.K. principles, complete pages in the activity book, and show your work to a ranger at the visitor center. After you make the B.A.R.K. Ranger promise, you and your doggo will get a Valley Forge B.A.R.K. Ranger dog tag!

Title: Hike Great Head with Island Explorer Bus
Short_Description: Great Head Trial provides visitors a chance to hike a shorter trail and get amazing views of the ocean and forest in the same hike. Enjoy the views while relaxing on the beach afterward.

Title: Hike Ocean Path Trail with Island Explorer Bus
Short_Description: Ocean Path follows atop Acadia’s granite cliffs along the Atlantic Ocean. While exploring the Ocean Path, visitors can enjoy scenic vistas and iconic park locations including Sand beach, Thunder Hole and Otter Cliffs. Combining this hike with Acadia’s Island Explorer bus gives hikers flexibility in trail distance on what would otherwise be an out-and-back hike.

Title: Fort Film: "I Am So Wore Out with Fatigue"
Short_Description: What was a typical day like for a Continental Soldier on the New York frontier after the Siege of 1777? Routine, work, and fear were common. As was doubt.  This 18 minute film tells the story of the garrison of Fort Schuyler from the perspective of a regular soldier.

Title: Get Technical
Short_Description: The combination of a working railroad yard and a world-class railroad museum gives visitors a chance to do more than just step back in time. Steamtown's Technology Museum is a visitor favorite, providing an immersive experience into the lights, sounds, and technology of the railroading industry.

Title: Millbrook Village
Short_Description: Take a walk around Millbrook Village.

Title: Easy Hike Along Hornbecks Creek
Short_Description: An easy, one-mile out and back hike along the Lower Hornbecks Creek will lead visitors to a small waterfall, but the waterfall is only part of the view!

Title: Explore the Roundhouse
Short_Description: The combination of a working railroad yard and a world-class railroad museum gives visitors a chance to do more than just step back in time. The Roundhouse has been adaptively rehabilitated to allow Steamtown's mechanics to care for the locomotives with light-duty maintenance and repairs.

Title: Colonial Dress-Up Trunk
Short_Description: You can dress up like a real colonial person during your visit to Fort Stanwix National Monument!

Title: Historic Fort Self-Guided Tour
Short_Description: A self-guided room tour guide can be found inside the park brochure or provided by a ranger upon entry to the park.

Title: Biking on the McDade Recreational Trail
Short_Description: Biking on the McDade Recreational Trail

Title: Snowshoe and Cross-country Ski on the McDade Trail
Short_Description: Snowshoeing and cross-country skiing on the McDade Recreational Trail.

Title: Hike Sargent and Penobscot Mountains from JPH with Island Explorer Bus
Short_Description: The trails up Sargent and Penobscot Mountains are located a short distance from the Jordan Pond House restaurant and gift shop.

Title: Hike Jordan Pond Loop with Island Explorer Bus
Short_Description: If looking for a way to relax, to take in some magnificent views, or to just burn some of the calories from the popovers consumed at the Jordan Pond house, the Jordan Pond Path is a great choice. Even just walking along the eastern shoreline is a certain way to enjoy some scenic vistas of one of Acadia’s iconic locations.

Title: Ride a Train
Short_Description: Steamtown's seasonal train rides offer a unique National Park experience. Rail experiences include short 30-minute trips exploring the park’s historic railroad yard.

Title: Bike The Battle Road Trail
Short_Description: The Battle Road Trail is a five mile trail connecting historic sites from Meriam’s Corner in Concord to the eastern boundary of the park in Lexington. Much of the trail follows the original 1775 road remnants: Where thousands of Colonial Militia and British Regulars fought a bloody engagement on April 19, 1775. Bicycling the trail is welcome and a great way to enjoy the park. The trail is accessible by several parking areas along Route 2A and Lexington Road.

Title: Hike the Cliff Trail
Short_Description: The Cliff Trial along the Raymondskill Ridge has gorgeous scenic views and just enough elevation gain to get the heart pumping.

Title: General Grant National Memorial Park Film
Short_Description: Watch the 22-minute park film that highlights Grant's life and accomplishments.

Title: Interpretive Exhibits
Short_Description: Contains a permanent exhibit gallery addressing some of the major events in Ulysses S. Grant's life, and this history of the mausoleum.

Title: Fort Stanwix B.A.R.K. Rangers!
Short_Description: Did you know that dogs are welcome to visit Fort Stanwix National Monument with their special people? Learn the rules of B.A.R.K., participate in two activities below, and take the oath to become a Bark Ranger! You can also visit the ranger station inside the historic fort for a special B.A.R.K. Ranger stamp!

Title: Hike the Brandywine Gorge Loop
Short_Description: The Brandywine Gorge Loop circles a deep ravine carved by Brandywine Creek, and provides stunning views of Brandywine Falls along the way. Learn more about hiking this popular trail.

Title: Hike To Barnard Mountain
Short_Description: Hike to the top of 1,558ft Barnard Mountain for a magnificent and expansive viewpoint of Katahdin Lake and Mt. Katahdin.

Title: Ranger Programs at Women's Rights
Short_Description: Ranger programs are offered at Women's Rights NHP year-round. Learn about the people and places that sparked a revolution for women's rights!

Title: Hike Gichi Onigaming (Grand Portage)
Short_Description: Several options exist for exploring the Grand Portage. From a canoe adventure starting in the Boundary Waters to a short walk from the Depot, this trail offers year-round options for hiking, portaging, skiing, and snowshoeing.

Title: Dreams of Equality Film & Museum
Short_Description: Begin your visit to Women's Rights National Historical Park with information, exhibits, and our park bookstore.

Title: Stony Man
Short_Description: MILE 41.7 | Pets are not allowed on this hike. This enjoyable hike brings you to an outstanding view of the Shenandoah Valley, Massanutten Mountain, and beyond.

Title: Horseback Riding from the Welcome Station
Short_Description: Enjoy the beauty of Valle Grande as you trek down the entrance road on your own horse.

Title: Fish the Upper Jaramillo Creek
Short_Description: The upper stretch of Jaramillo Creek offers a challenge for anglers with the reward of catching some truly large brown trout. The creek meanders through the lush Valle Jaramillo, which creates pools and overhanging banks for trout to hide in.

Title: Ski the Cerro La Jara Loop
Short_Description: This 1.5-mile (2.4 km) ski loop circles Cerro La Jara, the smallest volcanic dome in the park, which formed about 500,000 years ago when lava oozed up and onto the caldera floor.

Title: Backpacking Big Bend
Short_Description: Big Bend National Park is a backpacker's paradise. For those who come prepared, there are hundreds of miles of trails, and vast expanses to explore.

Title: Hike the Upper Falls Trail
Short_Description: Description of the Falls Trails.

Title: Hike the Tsankawi Loop Trail
Short_Description: Learn everything you need to know about hiking the Tsankawi Loop Trail.

Title: Fish the Middle Jaramillo Creek
Short_Description: Jaramillo Creek is the middle of the three fishable streams in Valles Caldera. The middle section of the creek runs from Cerro Pinon upstream to the Cerro Abrigo Trailhead.

Title: Visit the Anchorage Alaska Public Lands Information Center
Short_Description: Kick off your visit to Alaska with a stop at the Anchorage Alaska Public Lands Information Center. Get trip planning advice, explore exhibits, watch a park film, become a Jr Ranger and more!

Title: Drive the Red Route of the Yorktown Battlefield Auto Tour
Short_Description: Open year round until sunset, the Yorktown Battlefield Red Route Auto Tour is a 7 mile drive that takes visitors to the most important spots on the Yorktown Battlefield. Allow at least 45 minute to complete this tour. There are information waysides at each of the stops and along the route.

Title: Drive the Yellow Route of the Yorktown Battlefield Auto Tour
Short_Description: Open year round until sunset, the optional Yorktown Battlefield Yellow Route Auto Tour is a 9 mile drive that takes visitors through the Allied Encampment areas. . Allow at least 30 minute to complete this tour. There are information waysides at each of the stops and along the route.

Title: Watch "A Triumph of Tragedy: The Life of Charles Young"
Short_Description: Learn about the life and legacy of General Charles Young while viewing our 10 minute film. This is the park's official introductory film.

Title: Park Ranger Programs
Short_Description: Discover the vibrant history and community of Nicodemus through ranger programs! Request a guided tour of the town, plan a field trip with your school or group, or keep an eye out for special events throughout the year.

Title: A Contemplative Experience
Short_Description: Take in George Washington Carver’s words of wisdom while stopping to read the quote stones along the short, quarter mile Contemplative Loop Trail. Discover a sense of peace as GW Carver guides you through the woods.

Title: Ranger's Choice Programs
Short_Description: Daily programs being offered at Fort McHenry NM&HS!

Title: Visit the Frontlines of Freedom Exhibit
Short_Description: Explore the “Frontlines of Freedom” exhibit at Pinckney-Porter’s Chapel to learn about the legacy of Camp Saxton and the 1st South Carolina Volunteers.

Title: World War I Weekend at Eisenhower National Historic Site
Short_Description: Join Eisenhower National Historic Site and our living history partners as we commemorate the events of World War I and explore how that conflict shaped the United States and its future leaders like Dwight D. Eisenhower. This event will feature several living history displays, exhibits, and free tours of the Eisenhower home.

Title: Attend a George Washington Birthplace Ranger Program
Short_Description: Join a park ranger at the visitor center for a short half mile walking tour to explore the stories of this land from Colonial to Colonial Revival.

Title: Fishing at the Birthplace
Short_Description: George Washington Birthplace National Monument allows fishing as a means of providing for public enjoyment, and customary and traditional use, and regulates fishing to ensure that it is managed in a manner that avoids unacceptable impacts to park resources.

Title: Visiting with Pets
Short_Description: Discover where you can go with your pet on a visit to George Washington Birthplace National Monument.

Title: Picnicking at the Birthplace
Short_Description: Spending the day at the park? Bring a lunch and enjoy the scenery of the Picnic Area.

Title: Trails and Rails: Klondike Seattle
Short_Description: T&R guides provide programs on the Coast Starlight daily through Sunday, September 28, 2025.

Title: Tour the Sandburg Home
Short_Description: *Please call 828 693-4178 to ask about tour availability for same day visits. Limited tours may be available when staffing allows.* Each tour is 30-minutes, with a capacity of 10 people. (The only way to see the furnished interior of the Sandburg Home in person is through a guided tour.)

Title: Hike Around Bear Lake
Short_Description: Bear Lake is seen in winter and spring. This lake and the surrounding trail is typically covered with snow from late October through late May. Be prepared for snow and ice on the trail. Traction devices and waterproof boots are advised.

Title: Take a Lava Hike!
Short_Description: Many trails in the park were created in ancient times and others are from the historical era. These were trails for pack animals. The main park trail is a loop that circles the park and is 4.5 miles long or 7.2 kilometers. It is highly recommended to wear sturdy shoes, have sun protection and bring plenty of water. Many sections of the trail are rough and uneven and the ocean section can be effected by high tides and large surf.

Title: Hike to Alberta Falls
Short_Description: Glacier Creek thunders down this spectacular waterfall.

Title: Hike to Secret Beach and Skylight Cave
Short_Description: Skylight Cave is a sea cave with a collapsed ceiling at the north end of Secret Beach, which is located within the Phillip Burton Wilderness along the eastern shore of Drakes Bay. Even though Skylight Cave is only 2.5 miles (3.8 km) from the closest parking lot at Limantour Beach, it is usually only safe to visit at tides that measure -1 (negative one) foot or lower. The best thing you can do to guarantee a safe, fun visit to Secret Beach and Skylight Cave is to be prepared!

Title: Enjoy a Scenic Drive on Old Fall River Road
Short_Description: Built between 1913 and 1920, a drive up Old Fall River Road is an incredible 9.4-mile scenic drive. This unpaved road begins next to Endovalley Picnic Area and ends at the summit of Fall River Pass and in the parking area for Alpine Visitor Center. At Alpine Visitor Center, use Trail Ridge Road to return to the east side of RMNP.

Title: Oceanside Trail
Short_Description: The Oceanside Trail significantly increases recreational opportunities by providing an entirely new trail that increases the park’s trail network by over 20% while also offering a car-free mode of access to the tidepool area.

Title: Ranger Walkabouts
Short_Description: During the months of June, July, and August, join a Park Ranger on 60-minute walking tour at varying locations in the Blackstone Valley.

Title: Become an Oxon Hill Farm Junior Ranger
Short_Description: Oxon Hill Farm Junior Ranger Program

Title: Hike to Bumpass Hell
Short_Description: Hike the three-mile, round-trip trail to the largest hydrothermal area in the park.

Title: Hike Grand Portage Trails
Short_Description: Hike the Mount Rose Trail for stunning views of Grand Portage Bay and the Depot. To experience the trek the Voyageurs made as well as Indigenous people before them, hike a portion or all of Gitchi Onagamiing, the Grand Portage Trail.

Title: Petroglyphs in the Park!
Short_Description: The petroglyphs of Honokōhau are unique. A master craftsman laboring with stone tools and human hands in the hot sun made these into distinctive works of art that have become reservoirs of information. This area is a boardwalk that is along the mauka-makai trail a half mile from the visitor center or it can be accessed from the beach area (follow the signs to the visitor center).

Title: Report for Duty
Short_Description: Designed for visitors 5-12 (but open to all!) the Junior Ranger program is designed to help young visitors learn more about the people, places and events related to St. Louis history and westward expansion in the 1800s.

Title: Bike the Bear Valley Trail to the Glen Trail Junction
Short_Description: This wide gravel trail through Douglas fir forest is great for a family cruise, a mellow ride, or the start of a multi-sport adventure! This trail begins at the Bear Valley Trailhead, at the south end of the Bear Valley Parking Lot.

Title: Bike the Estero Trail
Short_Description: This trail through grassland and cattle pastures offers outstanding views of Drakes Estero and Estero de Limantour, and the opportunity to see the birds, bat rays, and leopard sharks that call these ecosystems home. Allow 25 minutes driving time from Bear Valley to the Estero Trailhead.

Title: Bike to Wildcat Campground
Short_Description: Biking to Wildcat Campground is no easy feat—but a lot of work yields a lot of reward! Enjoy your lunch at sunny Wildcat Campground or spend some time at Wildcat Beach after you bike up and over one of the highest points of Inverness Ridge. This ride starts at the Five Brooks Trailhead. Allow 10 minutes drive time from Bear Valley to the Five Brooks.

Title: Bike to Sky Campground
Short_Description: Ride your bike up a moderate incline along this multi-use trail through Douglas fir forest to Sky Campground, where you can enjoy a break with oceans views. Allow ten minutes driving time from Bear Valley to the Sky Trailhead, which is located along Limantour Road.

Title: NISI Junior Ranger Program
Short_Description: You can earn a Junior Ranger badge at the park and from home.

Title: Take a walk through time to the bank of the Potomac River
Short_Description: Take a two-mile walk from the parking area to access the shoreline of the Potomac River to relax along the river, search for shark teeth, or enjoy a picnic at the overlook.

Title: Attend a Ranger Stroll
Short_Description: Join a ranger on an educational stroll.

Title: Cave Tour
Short_Description: Join a ranger for a 55-minute cave tour through the Timpanogos Cave system. Enjoy the cool, damp, air, marvel at the delicate, colorful, formations and learn about the million-year process that has created these unique caverns.

Title: View the Russell Cave Museum
Short_Description: Temporary exhibit on display

Title: View the Russell Cave Film
Short_Description: Russell Cave Film 7 minutes

Title: Stargazing in Paradise & Stevens Canyon
Short_Description: Learn how to safely star gaze in the Paradise and Stevens Canyon Road areas of Mount Rainier National Park.

Title: Visit a Mainland Park
Short_Description: Made up of 34 unique islands and peninsulas, the Boston Harbor Islands have something for everyone. Choose a mainland park and plan your adventure!

Title: Join a Stewardship Saturday Program
Short_Description: Join us for a Stewardship Saturday program! Work alongside park staff clearing invasive species, improving trails, and protecting important habitat areas.

Title: Explore Temporary Exhibits in the Longfellow House
Short_Description: Temporary exhibits in a gallery inside the historic house use the rich museum collections at the site, including objects usually in storage, to explore theme's related to the site's history. On exhibit beginning May 2025: "Washington’s Headquarters and the Memory of the American Revolution"

Title: Explore the Lay of the Land Exhibit
Short_Description: Learn about the Reconstruction era in Beaufort at beyond at the "Lay of the Land" exhibit in the park visitor center in the Old Beaufort Firehouse.

Title: Join a Star Party
Short_Description: Gaze at the heavens with the National Park Service and the Shenandoah Astronomical Society (SAS). Telescopes are provided, or you may bring your own. SAS members can teach you how to use it. Bring camp chairs or blankets.

Title: National Parks of Boston: Find Your Park Anywhere
Short_Description: Find your park anywhere, from your home to your backyard, with activities spanning the National Parks of Boston.

Title: Explore Fort Warren
Short_Description: Travel back in time and explore Fort Warren on Georges Island.

Title: Swim in Boston Harbor
Short_Description: Cool off by taking a dip in Boston Harbor! Swim in Spectacle's designated swimming area.

Title: Become a Trails & Rails Guide
Short_Description: Have fun riding the train and sharing St. Louis area history!

Title: Hike between Scott's Run Nature Preserve and Theodore Roosevelt Island
Short_Description: The ten-mile Potomac Heritage Trail within George Washington Memorial Parkway is a popular path, connecting at some places to neighborhood trails, Turkey Run picnic area and Chain Bridge. Here, though, we suggest a longer hike connecting with another PHT segment in Scott's Run Nature Preserve, managed by the Fairfax County Park Authority. A combination natural surface path and road walk along Live Oak Lane connects the two.

Title: Take a walk or bike ride on the Mount Vernon Trail
Short_Description: The Mount Vernon Trail winds alongside the Potomac River offering uninterrupted views of Washington, D.C.'s skyline. A run, walk, or cycle along the trail leads to all of the same sites that can be reached by car on the parallel section of the parkway.

Title: History at Sunset
Short_Description: History at Sunset is an annual series that focuses on areas rarely visited, stories rarely told, or familiar sites seen through a different lens. Park rangers lead each History at Sunset event, which may be an evening tour or special program in the park or the surrounding area.

Title: Oldfarm Video Tour
Short_Description: George B. Dorr worked tirelessly through the early 1900s to found what we know today as Acadia National Park. His former family estate, known as Oldfarm, is situated along the Compass Harbor Trail, less than a mile from downtown Bar Harbor on State Route 3. The short walk beneath forest canopies leads to foundation remnants of his former cottage, demolished in 1949, and ends at Dorr Point with views of Compass Harbor and the Porcupine Islands.

Title: Birding at Joshua Tree
Short_Description: Joshua Tree National Park offers a wide range of birds for birders to discover.

Title: Volunteer to complete the PHT in Northern Virginia
Short_Description: Volunteers are essential to complete the PHT network in Northern Virginia.

Title: Watch an Artillery Demonstration at Fort Washington Park
Short_Description: Ever wonder how Civil War cannons were fired? Visit the park the first Sunday of every month from May to November to watch the Fort Washington Guard demonstrate and explain the procedures used during the muzzle-loading era of American Artillery.

Title: Woodlawn Manor and Pope-Leighey House
Short_Description: Tour Woodlawn Manor and Pope-Leighey House in the George Washington Memorial Parkway Corridor. View the vast differences in architecture from the early 1800’s to 1900’s in a unique juxtaposition along Route 1.

Title: Laurel Highlands Day Hike to Youghiogheny Overlook
Short_Description: Take the 2.5-mile ascent to a large, flat topped rock formation overlooking the Youghiogheny River valley. Get a view of the Sugarloaf Knob and the gorges cutting into the ridge from side streams.

Title: Hike Along the Towpath Through the Paw Paw Tunnel
Short_Description: Walk through the 3,118-foot Paw Paw Tunnel, a significant engineering feature along Mile 155 of the C&O Canal towpath.

Title: Hike to Lexington Arch
Short_Description: Hike 5.6 miles through shrubland to see one of the largest limestone arches in the world. Find plenty of peace on this lesser traveled trail. Lexington Arch is also the only park trail that allows dogs, so bring your pet for an excursion to this remote corner of the park.

Title: Hike the Upper Strawberry Trail
Short_Description: The meadows of the Sage Steppe Loop combined with moderate difficulty ascending to a mountain saddle, the Upper Strawberry trail's 4.0 miles provides ample fall colors and plenty of running water.

Title: Hike the Sage Steppe Loop
Short_Description: Wildlife, wildflowers, escapes from the heat, and an easy trail to boot. The Sage Steppe Loop is both a wonderful introduction to the park and a great chance to get away from the crowds.

Title: Hike the Serviceberry Trail
Short_Description: The 3.2 mile Serviceberry Loop is a wonderful loop trail through serviceberry, rabbitbrush, and sagebrush, giving plentiful views of unique Great Basin geology. This moderate trail is a great introduction to the Snake Creek area and its unique offerings.

Title: Hike the Big Wash Trail
Short_Description: Big Wash is perhaps the most difficult and least travelled trail in the park. be prepared for a long 20.6 mile journey through shrubland and heat in the summer, but one of the most incredible experiences available with the isolation it brings.

Title: Hike to Johnson Lake from Snake Creek
Short_Description: Hike to the backcountry and isolated Johnson Lake. 8.0 miles round trip rewards visitors with a chance for isolation and exercise. This version of the route approaches from the Snake Creek Trailhead, but Johnson Lake is also accessible from the north via Baker Creek Trailhead.

Title: Hike to Snake Creek Overlook
Short_Description: The Snake Creek Overlook trail is a nice, short trail that will work one's legs with a stretch of elevation gain. This easy, 1.1 mile trip provides sweeping views of the lower valley and is a great start or end of the day

Title: Hike the Shoshone ADA Trail
Short_Description: The Shoshone ADA trail is a small section of one of Great Basin's longer hiking routes. This 0.1 mile stretch takes visitors to a wooden platform and fishing area right on Snake Creek. Caution: Access may be difficult for modified vehicles with low clearance.

Title: Hike the Snake Divide Route
Short_Description: Hike one of the more challenging and rewarding trails inside Great Basin National Park. This over 13 mile hike leads to the peak of a mountain, multiple bristlecone pine groves, and some of the most spectacular views in the park.

Title: Hike to Dead Lake
Short_Description: Hike the 5.1 mile Dead Lake Loop to see an ephemeral lake and gorgeous wildflowers and fall colors. Every time of year, Snake Creek has something to offer.

Title: Hike to Baker Lake
Short_Description: The hike to Baker Lake is a 10 mile round trip through pine forests and along a mountain stream. Baker Lake is one of the largest mountain lakes in Great Basin National Park. Nestled in a mountain cirque the beautiful lake frequently has snow on its rocky banks.

Title: Hike the Timber Creek Trail
Short_Description: An extended loop through the Baker Creek area, Timber Creek offers more meadows, woodland, and creeks. This 5.1 mile loop is surely worth a visit, especially when the Scenic Drive is closed.

Title: Hike to Johnson Lake from Baker Creek
Short_Description: Johnson Lake may be reached from either Baker Creek or Snake Creek. This trail offers a path to a pristine mountain lake and mining history in the Snake Range. Nearly 10 miles of trail equals good times and plenty of room.

Title: Backpack the Baker & Johnson Lakes Loop
Short_Description: The most popular backcountry route in the park, the Baker & Johnson Lakes Loop frequently sees campers, but still nothing compared to many other popular parks. This about 12 mile loop will take visitors by mountain lakes, glacial cirques, and a profound amount of isolation.

Title: Hike the Baker Creek Loop
Short_Description: An upper easy/low moderate hike, Baker Creek is often accessible or semi-accessible year round, making a viable alternative when roads close or parking lots are crowded. 3.3 miles makes for acceptable easy distance for most visitors, though challenging in the winter when deep snow is present.

Title: Hike Pole Canyon
Short_Description: Hike the Pole Canyon trail, moseying by streams, wildflowers, and dense conifers. The 6.6 mile round trip trail also treks through the Baker Creek and Grey Cliffs campgrounds, making it easy to start from a campsite without a drive.

Title: Hike the Lehman Creek Trail
Short_Description: Hike the Lehman Creek Trail in summer and fall for amazing colors and plenty of shade, or to access the higher elevation trails in the winter time. This 6.4 mile round trip journey travels through a varied forest showcasing a harder to see perspective of the many mountain peaks.
